revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kanzan-ji]][[ja:舘山寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kanzan-ji]][[ja:舘山寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kanzan-ji]][[ja:舘山寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:舘山寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Kanzanji.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}}; {{ill|Sōtō|qid=Q749085|1=Sōtō}}|prefecture={{ill|Chūō-ku|qid=Q117084743|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.76822721|N|137.61304596|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Akiha Gongen|qid=Q2680724|1=Akiba Gongen}}; {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|qid=Q416174|1=Ākāśagarbha}}}}
{{Unreferenced|date=31 August 2014}} [[File:Kanzanji.jpg|thumb|300px|Sōtō-shū Kanzanji and Kanzanji Onsen district]] '''Kanzanji''' (舘山寺) refers to a {{ill|Sōtō|en|Sōtō|qid=Q749085|lt=Sōtō Zen|1=Sōtō}} Buddhist temple and the surrounding area in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu|ja|Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|qid=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.
== Overview == The temple is said to have been founded by {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} (Kōbō Daishi) in 810 CE, making it a historically significant temple. It was originally a sub-temple of {{ill|Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine|en|Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine|qid=Q500413|lt=Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine|1=Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine}}.<ref name="index">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://kanzanji.net/index.html |title=Sōtō-shū Akihasan Kanzanji |publisher=Official Website |accessdate=22 April 2021}}</ref> The temple also houses a shrine to {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|en|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|qid=Q713021|lt=Atago Shrine (disambiguation)|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}}.<ref name="jinjacho">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4414226 |title=Atago Shrine |publisher=Shizuoka Shrine Association |accessdate=22 April 2021}}</ref>
Its {{ill|Honzon|en|Honzon|qid=Q11520160|lt=Honzon|1=Honzon}} is {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|en|Ākāśagarbha|qid=Q416174|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|1=Ākāśagarbha}}.<ref name="honzon">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://kanzanji.net/gohonzon.html |title=About the Honzon |publisher=Official Website |accessdate=22 April 2021}}</ref><ref name="kotoba_daiji">{{Cite Kotobank|word=Kanzanji |encyclopedia=Digital Daijisen Plus |accessdate=22 April 2021}}</ref> The shrine enshrines {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}} (Akiha Sanshaku Bō Daigongen) as its guardian deity.<ref name="honzon" /> A fire festival is held annually on 15 December, where fire-walking follows sutra recitations.<ref name="himatsuri">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://kanzanji.net/akihasan.html#himatsuri |title=What is Akihasan Faith? |publisher=Official Website |accessdate=22 April 2021}}</ref> General participants are also welcome to join the fire-walking.<ref name="himatsuri" /> The temple preserves a letter addressed to {{ill|Ikkyū|en|Ikkyū|qid=Q467613|lt=Ikkyū Sōjun|1=Ikkyū}} from his mother.<ref name="honzon" />
=== History === * '''810 CE (Kōnin 1):''' Founded by Kūkai as a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon-shū|1=Shingon Buddhism}} temple.<ref name="index" /> * '''1187 CE (Bunji 3):''' Destroyed by fire during wartime. Subsequently rebuilt by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}.<ref name="index" /> * '''1384 CE (Genchū 1):''' Destroyed again by fire during wartime.<ref name="index" /> * '''1362 CE (Jōji 1):''' Fujiwara no Motohide (later Ōsawa clan) arrived as the lord of Horie Castle, providing patronage as the castle’s prayer temple.<ref name="index" /> * '''1598 CE (Keichō 3):''' Received shuin (vermilion seal) land grant from {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}.<ref name="index" /> * '''1870 CE (Meiji 3):''' Abolished due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=Shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1970535}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|qid=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} policies.<ref name="index" /> * '''1890 CE (Meiji 23):''' Restored by inviting Priest Maki Taizen of Akihasan Akihaji. Converted to Sōtō-shū and renamed its mountain title from Chūreizan to Akihasan.<ref name="index" />
=== Kanzan === The temple and the adjacent Atago Shrine are located at the end of the onsen street, accessible via stairs. Behind these lies the low mountain {{ill|Kanzan|ja|舘山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanzan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which faces {{ill|Lake Hamana|en|Lake Hamana|qid=Q1150952|lt=Lake Hamana|1=Lake Hamana}} and features a promenade with various landmarks.<ref name="sansaku">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://kanzanji.net/sansaku.html |title=Kanzan Walks |publisher=Official Website |accessdate=22 April 2021}}</ref><ref name="kanko_map">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanzanji.gr.jp/wp-content/themes/kanzanji/img/map/map_2021_spring.pdf |format=pdf |title=Kanzanji Onsen Stroll Map |publisher=[https://www.kanzanji.gr.jp/ Kanzanji Onsen Tourism Association] |accessdate=22 April 2021}}</ref> * Anadai-shi<ref name="honzon" /><ref name="sansaku" /> * Enmusubi Jizō-son<ref name="honzon" /><ref name="sansaku" /> * Sei Kannon Bosatsu (Kanzanji Great Kannon)<ref name="honzon" /><ref name="sansaku" /> * Saigyō Rock<ref name="sansaku" /> * Tosaka Rock<ref name="sansaku" /> * Fujimi Rock<ref name="sansaku" /> * Observation Deck<ref name="sansaku" />
=== Access === * '''By Car:''' 15 minutes from {{ill|Hamamatsu Nishi Interchange|ja|浜松西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamamatsu Nishi Interchange|1=Hamamatsu-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557882}} on the {{ill|Tōmei Expressway|en|Tōmei Expressway|qid=Q1135694|lt=Tōmei Expressway|1=Tōmei Expressway}}, or closer via Kanzanji Smart Interchange.<ref name="access">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://kanzanji.net/access.html |title=Transport & Access |publisher=Official Website |accessdate=22 April 2021}}</ref> * '''By Bus:''' About 45 minutes from {{ill|Hamamatsu Station|en|Hamamatsu Station|qid=Q603640|lt=Hamamatsu Station|1=Hamamatsu Station}} on Entetsu Bus; alight at "Kanzanji Onsen" stop and walk 5 minutes.<ref name="access" />
=== Gallery === <gallery> File:Kanzanji atagojinja 20210102.jpg|Atago Shrine within the temple grounds File:Kanzanji atagojinja torii 20210102.jpg|Atago Shrine torii. The stairs leading to Kanzanji's main hall are to the right. File:Kanzanji Seikannon Bosatsu 01.JPG|Sei Kannon Bosatsu (built in 1937<ref name="sansaku" />), likened to former Prime Minister {{ill|Shinzo Abe|en|Shinzo Abe|qid=Q132345|lt=Shinzō Abe|1=Shinzo Abe}} by some media.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/113570 |title="Looks just like him": Abe Shinzō and Kanzanji Kannon Statue |publisher=Chunichi Shimbun |date=1 September 2020 |accessdate=6 September 2020}}</ref> </gallery>
== Kanzanji-chō == {{Pathnav|Japan|Shizuoka Prefecture|Hamamatsu City|Chūō-ku|'''Kanzanji-chō'''|hide=1|frame=1}} {{Infobox settlement | name = Kanzanji-chō | settlement_type = Town | image_skyline = Kanzanji-hotspring-town.jpg | imagesize = 300px | image_caption = Kanzanji Onsen district | image_map = | map_caption = Location of Kanzanji-chō | pushpin_map = Japan Shizuoka | latd = 34 | latm = 45 | lats = 39.69 | latNS = N | longd = 137 | longm = 36 | longs = 48.26 | longEW = E | subdivision_type = Country | subdivision_name = {{flagicon|JPN}} Japan | subdivision_type1 = Prefecture | subdivision_name1 = {{flagicon|Shizuoka Prefecture}} Shizuoka Prefecture | subdivision_type2 = City | subdivision_name2 = {{flagicon|Hamamatsu City}} Hamamatsu City | subdivision_type3 = Ward | subdivision_name3 = {{ill|Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu|ja|Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | established_title1 = Established | established_date1 = | area_footnotes = <ref name="menseki">{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/library/3_tochi-kisyou/documents/27-2_menseki-jinkou_area_kubetsu.pdf Land Statistics: Neighborhood Area Sizes (March 2010) - Hamamatsu City] |accessdate=14 December 2018}}</ref> | area_total_km2 = 2.2506 | population_as_of = 1 December 2018 | population_footnotes = <ref name="population">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/library/1_jinkou-setai/005_kubetsu.html |title=Population Statistics by Neighborhood |publisher=Hamamatsu City |accessdate=13 December 2018}}</ref> | population_total = 2542 | population_density_km2 = auto | timezone1 = JST | utc_offset1 = +9 | postal_code_type = Postal Code | postal_code = 431-1209<ref name="postal">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.post.japanpost.jp/cgi-zip/zipcode.php?zip=4311209 |title=Postal Code |publisher=Japan Post |accessdate=13 December 2018}}</ref> | area_code = 053 (Hamamatsu MA)<ref name="areacode">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.soumu.go.jp/main_sosiki/joho_tsusin/top/tel_number/shigai_list.html |title=List of Area Codes |publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications |accessdate=13 December 2018}}</ref> | registration_plate = Hamamatsu | footnotes = }} Located on the northeast shore of {{ill|Lake Hamana|en|Lake Hamana|qid=Q1150952|lt=Lake Hamana|1=Lake Hamana}} and featuring Uchūra Bay between Kanzanji-chō and Kusamatsu-chō, Kanzanji serves as a hub for tourism in the Hamamatsu and Lake Hamana area. The {{ill|Kanzanji Onsen Lantern Floating Fireworks Festival|ja|舘山寺温泉灯篭流し花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanzanji Onsen Lantern Floating Fireworks Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浜名湖かんざんじ温泉灯篭流し花火大会|1=Kanzanji Onsen Lantern Floating Fireworks Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85881965}} is held here every summer. In 2004, the {{ill|Lake Hamana Expo|ja|浜名湖花博|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Hamana Expo|1=Pacific Flora 2004|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557606}} was hosted in nearby Murakushi-chō, further enhancing its appeal as a tourist center.
=== Kanzanji Onsen === The {{ill|Kanzanji Onsen|ja|舘山寺温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanzanji Onsen|1=Kanzanji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11613539}} was discovered in 1958 and is known for its therapeutic effects on fatigue, neuralgia, and poor circulation.
=== Facilities and Attractions === {{see also|Kanzanji Onsen}} * {{ill|Kanzanji Ropeway|en|Kanzanji Ropeway|qid=Q6365942|lt=Kanzanji Ropeway|1=Kanzanji Ropeway}} * {{ill|Hamamatsu City Zoo|ja|浜松市動物園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamamatsu City Zoo|1=Hamamatsu Zoological Gardens|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557788}} * {{ill|Hamamatsu City Flower Park|ja|浜松市フラワーパーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamamatsu City Flower Park|1=Hamamatsu Flower Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9055804}} * {{ill|Hamanako Kanzanji Art Museum|ja|浜名湖舘山寺美術博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanako Kanzanji Art Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hamanako Music Box Museum|ja|浜名湖オルゴールミュージアム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanako Music Box Museum|1=Hamanako Orgel Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557595}} (in Kusamatsu-chō) * {{ill|Hamanako Pal Pal|ja|浜名湖パルパル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanako Pal Pal|1=Hamanako Palpal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557598}}

=== Education === Children in Kanzanji-chō attend the following public schools:<ref name="school">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/kyoiku/kyoiku/kuiki/index.html |title=School Districts |publisher=Hamamatsu City |accessdate=13 December 2018}}</ref> {| class="wikitable" !Area!!Elementary School!!Middle School |- ||Entire Area||Shōnai Elementary School||Shōnai Middle School |}
=== Postal Information === * Postal Code: 431-1209<ref name="postal" /> (Mail handled by Izaji Post Office)<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/zipcode/dl/bangobo/zip_bgb02.pdf Postal Code Directory, 2017 Edition - Japan Post] |accessdate=13 December 2018}}</ref>
=== Policing === Law enforcement in Kanzanji-chō is managed as follows:<ref name="police">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.police.pref.shizuoka.jp/keisatusho/hamachu/kobantop/kobantop.html |title=Police Stations |publisher=Hamamatsu Central Police Station |accessdate=13 December 2018}}</ref> {| class="wikitable" !Area!!Police Station!!Kōban/Substation |- ||Entire Area||Hamamatsu West Police Station||Kanzanji Town Kōban |}

==== Rail ==== Kanzanji Ropeway: Kanzanji Station (towards Ōkusan Station)
==== Bus ==== * Entetsu Bus ''' Route 30 Kanzanji Line: (towards Hamamatsu Station) - Higashibōzuka - Zoo - Hamanako Bay Street - Well Season Hamanako - Kanzanji Onsen - Kanzanji Town - Kanzanji South - Umagatari South (towards Murakushi or Hamanako Garden Park)

''The World of Variant Kanji Characters''. {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Kanzanji.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}}; {{ill|Sōtō|qid=Q749085|1=Sōtō}}|prefecture={{ill|Chūō-ku|qid=Q117084743|1=Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.76822721|N|137.61304596|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Akiha Gongen|qid=Q2680724|1=Akiba Gongen}}; {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|qid=Q416174|1=Ākāśagarbha}}}}
{{Unreferenced|date=31 August 2014}} [[File:Kanzanji.jpg|thumb|300px|Sōtō-shū Kanzanji and Kanzanji Onsen district]] '''Kanzanji''' (舘山寺) refers to a {{ill|Sōtō|en|Sōtō|qid=Q749085|lt=Sōtō Zen|1=Sōtō}} Buddhist temple and the surrounding area in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu|ja|Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|qid=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.
== Overview == The temple is said to have been founded by {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} (Kōbō Daishi) in 810 CE, making it a historically significant temple. It was originally a sub-temple of {{ill|Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine|en|Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine|qid=Q500413|lt=Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine|1=Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine}}.<ref name="index">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://kanzanji.net/index.html |title=Sōtō-shū Akihasan Kanzanji |publisher=Official Website |accessdate=22 April 2021}}</ref> The temple also houses a shrine to {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|en|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|qid=Q713021|lt=Atago Shrine (disambiguation)|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}}.<ref name="jinjacho">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4414226 |title=Atago Shrine |publisher=Shizuoka Shrine Association |accessdate=22 April 2021}}</ref>
Its {{ill|Honzon|en|Honzon|qid=Q11520160|lt=Honzon|1=Honzon}} is {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|en|Ākāśagarbha|qid=Q416174|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|1=Ākāśagarbha}}.<ref name="honzon">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://kanzanji.net/gohonzon.html |title=About the Honzon |publisher=Official Website |accessdate=22 April 2021}}</ref><ref name="kotoba_daiji">{{Cite Kotobank|word=Kanzanji |encyclopedia=Digital Daijisen Plus |accessdate=22 April 2021}}</ref> The shrine enshrines {{ill|Akiba Gongen|ja|秋葉権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Gongen|qq=}} (Akiha Sanshaku Bō Daigongen) as its guardian deity.<ref name="honzon" /> A fire festival is held annually on 15 December, where fire-walking follows sutra recitations.<ref name="himatsuri">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://kanzanji.net/akihasan.html#himatsuri |title=What is Akihasan Faith? |publisher=Official Website |accessdate=22 April 2021}}</ref> General participants are also welcome to join the fire-walking.<ref name="himatsuri" /> The temple preserves a letter addressed to {{ill|Ikkyū|en|Ikkyū|qid=Q467613|lt=Ikkyū Sōjun|1=Ikkyū}} from his mother.<ref name="honzon" />
=== History === * '''810 CE (Kōnin 1):''' Founded by Kūkai as a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon-shū|1=Shingon Buddhism}} temple.<ref name="index" /> * '''1187 CE (Bunji 3):''' Destroyed by fire during wartime. Subsequently rebuilt by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}.<ref name="index" /> * '''1384 CE (Genchū 1):''' Destroyed again by fire during wartime.<ref name="index" /> * '''1362 CE (Jōji 1):''' Fujiwara no Motohide (later Ōsawa clan) arrived as the lord of Horie Castle, providing patronage as the castle’s prayer temple.<ref name="index" /> * '''1598 CE (Keichō 3):''' Received shuin (vermilion seal) land grant from {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}.<ref name="index" /> * '''1870 CE (Meiji 3):''' Abolished due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=Shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1970535}} and {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|qid=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} policies.<ref name="index" /> * '''1890 CE (Meiji 23):''' Restored by inviting Priest Maki Taizen of Akihasan Akihaji. Converted to Sōtō-shū and renamed its mountain title from Chūreizan to Akihasan.<ref name="index" />
=== Kanzan === The temple and the adjacent Atago Shrine are located at the end of the onsen street, accessible via stairs. Behind these lies the low mountain {{ill|Kanzan|ja|舘山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanzan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which faces {{ill|Lake Hamana|en|Lake Hamana|qid=Q1150952|lt=Lake Hamana|1=Lake Hamana}} and features a promenade with various landmarks.<ref name="sansaku">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://kanzanji.net/sansaku.html |title=Kanzan Walks |publisher=Official Website |accessdate=22 April 2021}}</ref><ref name="kanko_map">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanzanji.gr.jp/wp-content/themes/kanzanji/img/map/map_2021_spring.pdf |format=pdf |title=Kanzanji Onsen Stroll Map |publisher=[https://www.kanzanji.gr.jp/ Kanzanji Onsen Tourism Association] |accessdate=22 April 2021}}</ref> * Anadai-shi<ref name="honzon" /><ref name="sansaku" /> * Enmusubi Jizō-son<ref name="honzon" /><ref name="sansaku" /> * Sei Kannon Bosatsu (Kanzanji Great Kannon)<ref name="honzon" /><ref name="sansaku" /> * Saigyō Rock<ref name="sansaku" /> * Tosaka Rock<ref name="sansaku" /> * Fujimi Rock<ref name="sansaku" /> * Observation Deck<ref name="sansaku" />
=== Access === * '''By Car:''' 15 minutes from {{ill|Hamamatsu Nishi Interchange|ja|浜松西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamamatsu Nishi Interchange|1=Hamamatsu-nishi interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557882}} on the {{ill|Tōmei Expressway|en|Tōmei Expressway|qid=Q1135694|lt=Tōmei Expressway|1=Tōmei Expressway}}, or closer via Kanzanji Smart Interchange.<ref name="access">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://kanzanji.net/access.html |title=Transport & Access |publisher=Official Website |accessdate=22 April 2021}}</ref> * '''By Bus:''' About 45 minutes from {{ill|Hamamatsu Station|en|Hamamatsu Station|qid=Q603640|lt=Hamamatsu Station|1=Hamamatsu Station}} on Entetsu Bus; alight at "Kanzanji Onsen" stop and walk 5 minutes.<ref name="access" />
=== Gallery === <gallery> File:Kanzanji atagojinja 20210102.jpg|Atago Shrine within the temple grounds File:Kanzanji atagojinja torii 20210102.jpg|Atago Shrine torii. The stairs leading to Kanzanji's main hall are to the right. File:Kanzanji Seikannon Bosatsu 01.JPG|Sei Kannon Bosatsu (built in 1937<ref name="sansaku" />), likened to former Prime Minister {{ill|Shinzo Abe|en|Shinzo Abe|qid=Q132345|lt=Shinzō Abe|1=Shinzo Abe}} by some media.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/113570 |title="Looks just like him": Abe Shinzō and Kanzanji Kannon Statue |publisher=Chunichi Shimbun |date=1 September 2020 |accessdate=6 September 2020}}</ref> </gallery>
== Kanzanji-chō == {{Pathnav|Japan|Shizuoka Prefecture|Hamamatsu City|Chūō-ku|'''Kanzanji-chō'''|hide=1|frame=1}} {{Infobox settlement | name = Kanzanji-chō | settlement_type = Town | image_skyline = Kanzanji-hotspring-town.jpg | imagesize = 300px | image_caption = Kanzanji Onsen district | image_map = | map_caption = Location of Kanzanji-chō | pushpin_map = Japan Shizuoka | latd = 34 | latm = 45 | lats = 39.69 | latNS = N | longd = 137 | longm = 36 | longs = 48.26 | longEW = E | subdivision_type = Country | subdivision_name = {{flagicon|JPN}} Japan | subdivision_type1 = Prefecture | subdivision_name1 = {{flagicon|Shizuoka Prefecture}} Shizuoka Prefecture | subdivision_type2 = City | subdivision_name2 = {{flagicon|Hamamatsu City}} Hamamatsu City | subdivision_type3 = Ward | subdivision_name3 = {{ill|Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu|ja|Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūō-ku, Hamamatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | established_title1 = Established | established_date1 = | area_footnotes = <ref name="menseki">{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/library/3_tochi-kisyou/documents/27-2_menseki-jinkou_area_kubetsu.pdf Land Statistics: Neighborhood Area Sizes (March 2010) - Hamamatsu City] |accessdate=14 December 2018}}</ref> | area_total_km2 = 2.2506 | population_as_of = 1 December 2018 | population_footnotes = <ref name="population">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/gyousei/library/1_jinkou-setai/005_kubetsu.html |title=Population Statistics by Neighborhood |publisher=Hamamatsu City |accessdate=13 December 2018}}</ref> | population_total = 2542 | population_density_km2 = auto | timezone1 = JST | utc_offset1 = +9 | postal_code_type = Postal Code | postal_code = 431-1209<ref name="postal">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.post.japanpost.jp/cgi-zip/zipcode.php?zip=4311209 |title=Postal Code |publisher=Japan Post |accessdate=13 December 2018}}</ref> | area_code = 053 (Hamamatsu MA)<ref name="areacode">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.soumu.go.jp/main_sosiki/joho_tsusin/top/tel_number/shigai_list.html |title=List of Area Codes |publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications |accessdate=13 December 2018}}</ref> | registration_plate = Hamamatsu | footnotes = }} Located on the northeast shore of {{ill|Lake Hamana|en|Lake Hamana|qid=Q1150952|lt=Lake Hamana|1=Lake Hamana}} and featuring Uchūra Bay between Kanzanji-chō and Kusamatsu-chō, Kanzanji serves as a hub for tourism in the Hamamatsu and Lake Hamana area. The {{ill|Kanzanji Onsen Lantern Floating Fireworks Festival|ja|舘山寺温泉灯篭流し花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanzanji Onsen Lantern Floating Fireworks Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浜名湖かんざんじ温泉灯篭流し花火大会|1=Kanzanji Onsen Lantern Floating Fireworks Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85881965}} is held here every summer. In 2004, the {{ill|Lake Hamana Expo|ja|浜名湖花博|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Hamana Expo|1=Pacific Flora 2004|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557606}} was hosted in nearby Murakushi-chō, further enhancing its appeal as a tourist center.
=== Kanzanji Onsen === The {{ill|Kanzanji Onsen|ja|舘山寺温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanzanji Onsen|1=Kanzanji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11613539}} was discovered in 1958 and is known for its therapeutic effects on fatigue, neuralgia, and poor circulation.
=== Facilities and Attractions === {{see also|Kanzanji Onsen}} * {{ill|Kanzanji Ropeway|en|Kanzanji Ropeway|qid=Q6365942|lt=Kanzanji Ropeway|1=Kanzanji Ropeway}} * {{ill|Hamamatsu City Zoo|ja|浜松市動物園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamamatsu City Zoo|1=Hamamatsu Zoological Gardens|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557788}} * {{ill|Hamamatsu City Flower Park|ja|浜松市フラワーパーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamamatsu City Flower Park|1=Hamamatsu Flower Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9055804}} * {{ill|Hamanako Kanzanji Art Museum|ja|浜名湖舘山寺美術博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanako Kanzanji Art Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Hamanako Music Box Museum|ja|浜名湖オルゴールミュージアム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanako Music Box Museum|1=Hamanako Orgel Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557595}} (in Kusamatsu-chō) * {{ill|Hamanako Pal Pal|ja|浜名湖パルパル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanako Pal Pal|1=Hamanako Palpal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557598}}
=== Population === As of 1 December 2018, Kanzanji-chō's population and household count were as follows:<ref name="population" /> {| class="wikitable" !Neighborhood!!Households!!Population |- |Kanzanji-chō |style="text-align:right"|1,102 |style="text-align:right"|2,542 |}
=== Education === Children in Kanzanji-chō attend the following public schools:<ref name="school">{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/kyoiku/kyoiku/kuiki/index.html |title=School Districts |publisher=Hamamatsu City |accessdate=13 December 2018}}</ref> {| class="wikitable" !Area!!Elementary School!!Middle School |- ||Entire Area||Shōnai Elementary School||Shōnai Middle School |}
=== Postal Information === * Postal Code: 431-1209<ref name="postal" /> (Mail handled by Izaji Post Office)<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/zipcode/dl/bangobo/zip_bgb02.pdf Postal Code Directory, 2017 Edition - Japan Post] |accessdate=13 December 2018}}</ref>
=== Policing === Law enforcement in Kanzanji-chō is managed as follows:<ref name="police">{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.police.pref.shizuoka.jp/keisatusho/hamachu/kobantop/kobantop.html |title=Police Stations |publisher=Hamamatsu Central Police Station |accessdate=13 December 2018}}</ref> {| class="wikitable" !Area!!Police Station!!Kōban/Substation |- ||Entire Area||Hamamatsu West Police Station||Kanzanji Town Kōban |}
=== Transport ===
==== Rail ==== Kanzanji Ropeway: Kanzanji Station (towards Ōkusan Station)
==== Bus ==== * Entetsu Bus ''' Route 30 Kanzanji Line: (towards Hamamatsu Station) - Higashibōzuka - Zoo - Hamanako Bay Street - Well Season Hamanako - Kanzanji Onsen - Kanzanji Town - Kanzanji South - Umagatari South (towards Murakushi or Hamanako Garden Park)
==== Roads ====
===== Prefectural Routes ===== * Shizuoka Prefectural Route 48 (Kanzanji-Kanaya Line) * Shizuoka Prefectural Route 320 (Inasa-Kanzanji Line) * Shizuoka Prefectural Route 323 (Kanzanji-Bentenjima Line)
== Notes == [[File:Kanzan Ji Keshiki cropped Tōtōmi Koseki Zue Frame 12.jpg|thumb|200px|An Edo-period reference to "館山寺" in {{ill|Tōtōmi Koseki Zue|ja|遠江古蹟図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Koseki Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (edited by Fuji Chōkō, 1803, National Diet Library Collection)]] When written in kanji, Kanzanji-chō is officially rendered with the common character "舘." However, alternative renderings using the standard "館" are often seen, including on signage for the Hamamatsu Nishi Interchange and Kanzanji Bus Stop on the Tōmei Expressway.<ref>Koike, Kazuo. ''The World of Variant Kanji Characters''. Kawade Shobō Shinsha, 2013, p. 117.</ref><ref>Hamamatsu City Office, "[https://www.city.hamamatsu.shizuoka.jp/shiminkyodo/intro/dmc/kuwari/zentyoumei1.html Neighborhood Name and Postal Code List]" (2020-01-01).</ref>
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Flower Bus (Hamamatsu)|ja|フラワー号 (浜松市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flower Bus (Hamamatsu)|1=Flower Bus (Hamamatsu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11333611}} - A community bus service in Kanzanji Onsen * {{ill|Kanzanji Onsen Tourism Association|ja|舘山寺温泉観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanzanji Onsen Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kanzanji Onsen Lantern Floating Fireworks Festival|ja|舘山寺温泉灯篭流し花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanzanji Onsen Lantern Floating Fireworks Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浜名湖かんざんじ温泉灯篭流し花火大会|1=Kanzanji Onsen Lantern Floating Fireworks Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85881965}} - A summer event featuring lantern floating and fireworks * ''Seibu Keisatsu Part-II'' (Western Police, Part II) - Featured Kanzanji in episodes 10-11 during a 1982 nationwide location shoot
